title: fix(gen-text): add stripCacheControl transform to grok models
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `transform: stripCacheControl` to `grok`, `grok-large`, `grok-4.3` (all on the same Azure Myceli endpoint).
- These were the only Azure/strict-OpenAI-compatible text models missing the transform that `mistral`, `kimi`/`kimi-k2.6`, `glm` already carry.
- Without it, `cache_control` annotations in multi-turn requests are rejected/mishandled by the Azure-deployed Grok upstream, dropping conversation history. Stripping them restores multi-turn context.
- Extends `stripCacheControl.test.ts` to assert all three grok entries wire up the transform (6/6 pass).

Risks: strips `cache_control` annotations for grok like sibling Azure models; no effect on providers that already ignored them.

Fixes #10722

gist.headline: Grok gets its memory back
gist.blurb: Grok models were getting a little amnesiac during long conversations. We've stripped out some problematic cache headers so they can actually remember what you said five minutes ago.
gist.summary: Added the stripCacheControl transform to grok, grok-large, and grok-4.3 models to prevent upstream Azure endpoints from rejecting cache_control annotations.
gist.impact: Multi-turn conversations with Grok models now correctly retain their history instead of dropping context.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee helping the Monitor Robot remember a conversation, represented by a chain of connected chat bubbles or a glowing memory disk.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot handing a glowing memory floppy disk to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head. The monitor displays a continuous chain of chat bubbles. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
